overview: A single lightweight backend service exposing a simple REST API on port 8080 that returns the current UTC timestamp in RFC3339 format. The service is stateless, requires no external dependencies, and handles GET requests to /time with JSON responses.
requirements: The service must expose an HTTP endpoint at GET /time that returns the current UTC timestamp
The timestamp must be returned in RFC3339 format for easy parsing
The service must listen on port 8080
The service must return responses in JSON format
The service must include a /health endpoint for health checks
The service must be stateless and require no external storage or dependencies

components.name: time-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Time Service API
  description: A lightweight service that returns the current UTC time in RFC3339 format
  version: 1.0.0
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:8080
    description: Local development server
paths:
  /time:
    get:
      summary: Get current UTC time
      description: Returns the current UTC timestamp in RFC3339 format
      operationId: getCurrentTime
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Successfully retrieved current time
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  time:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
                    description: Current UTC time in RFC3339 format
                    example: '2024-01-15T14:30:45Z'
                required:
                  - time
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  error:
                    type: string
                    description: Error message
                    example: 'Internal server error'
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      description: Returns the health status of the service
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: 'ok'
                required:
                  - status
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go service using net/http that listens on port 8080. Create two HTTP handlers: 1) GET /time - return current UTC time using time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339) wrapped in a JSON object with key 'time'. 2) GET /health - return JSON object with status 'ok'. Use encoding/json for JSON marshaling. Set Content-Type header to 'application/json' for all responses. Handle errors gracefully and return 500 status with error JSON on failures. Use http.ListenAndServe on :8080. Structure: main.go with timeHandler and healthHandler functions. No external dependencies beyond standard library. Dockerfile should use golang:1.21-alpine as builder, compile the binary, then use alpine:latest as runtime. Expose port 8080. No configuration files needed. The service is stateless with no database or cache requirements.
